release_notes: Fixes a slow memory leak on macOS that could cause the app to unexpectedly stop after several hours of continuous uptime. The native menu-bar status-item pump runs every 200 ms (~430,000 times per day) and was creating an autoreleased NSDate on every tick without ever draining the autorelease pool — because Tkinter drives the main loop, not AppKit, so no pool drain happens between iterations. Over hours, accumulated Cocoa objects exhausted the process's virtual address space and macOS aborted the process with SIGABRT (kernel triage: 'mach_vm_allocate_kernel failed within call to vm_map_enter'). _pump_macos_runloop and its initial-pump counterpart now wrap each runUntilDate_ call in objc.autorelease_pool(), draining the pool every tick. Windows behavior is unchanged — the affected code path is gated on sys.platform == 'darwin' and never executes on Windows builds, which use pystray + Win32 messaging instead.
